retrieved_docs.title: Mohs Micrographic Surgery: Evaluation and Treatment of Nonmelanoma Skin Cancer -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Chronic and intense sun exposure is the primary risk factor for developing NMSC. SCC arises from UV radiation-induced DNA damage in keratinocytes within the epidermis, leading to the formation of thymidine dimers and mutations in the TP53 gene. TP53 , a tumor suppressor gene, plays a crucial role in inducing apoptosis of damaged or abnormal keratinocytes. [8] Without TP53 , often called the “guardian of the genome,” mutated keratinocytes can proliferate, progressing to actinic keratoses (precancerous growths) or cutaneous SCC. Similarly, BCC results from prolonged UV exposure, frequently involving mutations in the PTCH1 gene. When PTCH1 is mutated, it fails to block the smoothened (SMO) protein, allowing activation of Gli transcription factors and leading to the unchecked growth characteristic of BCC. [Sauer's Manual of Skin Diseases] This mutation drives the uncontrolled proliferation of mutated cells. Both SCC and BCC underscore the significant role of UV radiation in their pathogenesis, though each has distinct underlying genetic mechanisms. [9] [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Vismodegib -- Mechanism of Action
retrieved_docs.content: The Hedgehog pathway regulates cell growth and differentiation in embryogenesis. This pathway is not active in adult tissue. PTCH1 is a tumor suppressor gene involved in the SHH pathway. SMO is a G-protein-coupled transmembrane receptor involved in signal transduction of the SHH pathway. Mutations in the smoothened (SMO) and PTCH1 genes appear to lead to abnormal up-regulation and activation of the SHH signaling pathway. Protein patched homolog 1 (Patched) is produced by the gene PTCH1 and functions as a transmembrane receptor that, when activated, suppresses the migration of the SMO receptor to the cell membrane. When the SHH ligand binds to Patched, the SMO receptor is released into the cell membrane, after which activation of cell proliferation occurs. In basal cell carcinoma, mutations in the Hedgehog pathway can result in an unrestricted proliferation of basal cells; researchers have noted that this pathway is relevant in more than 90% of basal cell carcinoma cases. One of the mechanisms believed to be involved in tumorigenesis involves ligand-independent pathway activation by SMO.

retrieved_docs.content: The WHP is very complex and involves high levels of coordination between multiple tissues and cell types [6] . Consequently, impairment in the process can occur at any step along the sequence that leads to process completion. Many known factors can affect or modulate wound healing [7] . In the subsequent sections, we will discuss major modulators (both positive and negative) of the WHP, including a summary of some of the methods and techniques devised to promote wound healing [9] . retrieved_docs.title: Wound Healing Phases -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Current studies on wound healing focus on identifying molecular level target genes that can be enhanced to expedite natural wound healing. The hedgehog signaling pathway has been used in multiple studies due to its role in epithelial-mesenchymal interaction in wound healing. [8] Laser techniques are being explored to enhance cell proliferation and accelerate wound healing. [9] [10] A multifaceted approach to wound healing focusing on adequate dressing and local care, nutritional support, and hyperbaric oxygen therapy in severe cases is necessary to ensure proper wound healing in the most difficult of cases.

retrieved_docs.title: Embryology, Esophagus -- Biochemical -- Hedgehog Signaling
retrieved_docs.content: Hedgehog is another potent mediator that plays critical roles in the development of the esophagus and other structures. Aberrant activation of hedgehog correlates with numerous developmental disorders and cancers of the brain and GI tracts. Of the major components of hedgehog signaling, Sonic hedgehog (Shh) seems to play the most prominent role in the esophagus and tracheal organogenesis. [2] [18] Development and differentiation of the esophagus and trachea require continual interaction between mesenchymal and endodermal components for proper development to occur. Shh's role is dose-dependent, highly regulated, and positional dependent. Shh initiates cascades which feature several other mediators, most prominently fibroblastic growth factor (FGF), transforming growth factor beta-bone morphogenic protein (TGF beta-BMP), Wnt, retinoic acid, and Notch signaling. [2] [3] [18] Shh's role appears to be regulated by post-transcriptional modifications, including phosphorylations and palmitoylations, as well as several different receptors to which Shh can bind on the target cell. Modifications of Shh determine which signaling cascade is activated and, thus, downstream gene expression. [2] [3] A key receptor indicated in foregut differentiation and lung bud formation includes the zinc finger glioma-associated transcription factors, which, when bound to Shh, activates gene transcription. However, in the absence of Shh, zinc finger glioma-associated transcription factors act as repressors adding an additional dimension to the location-dependent effects of Shh on organogenesis. [11] [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Mohs Micrographic Surgery, Optimizing Wound Healing -- Function -- Proliferation
retrieved_docs.content: In the subsequent phase, fibroblasts proliferate and migrate into the wound bed, synthesizing extracellular matrix components such as collagen to restore tissue integrity. Fibroblasts are activated by growth factors, including fibroblast growth factor (FGF) and TGF-β, leading to their differentiation into myofibroblasts, which express α-smooth muscle actin (α-SMA) and contribute to wound contraction. Additionally, keratinocytes at the wound edges proliferate and migrate across the wound bed, a process facilitated by matrix metalloproteinases (MMPs) that degrade the provisional matrix. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Granulation Tissue -- Cellular Level -- Keratinocytes
retrieved_docs.content: Keratinocytes are responsible for re-epithelializing the epidermis after injury. Their ability to differentiate and proliferate depends on the stem cell population at the wound site and stimulation by various cytokines and growth factors (epidermal growth factor, TGF-α, and keratinocyte growth factor [KGF]). The cell-to-cell binding between keratinocytes is looser during wound healing and strengthens after completion. Immediately after injury, the keratinocytes adjacent to the wound undergo an activation process that alters gene expression and promotes migration over the wound bed.

retrieved_docs.title: Wound Healing Phases -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: Wounds generally heal in 4 to 6 weeks. Chronic wounds are those that fail to heal within this timeframe.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 2052
retrieved_docs.content: ENZYMES FOR TREATMENT OF WOUNDS AND ULCERS are classified as a type of PREPARATIONS FOR TREATMENT OF WOUNDS AND ULCERS. The broader category PREPARATIONS FOR TREATMENT OF WOUNDS AND ULCERS includes CICATRICANTS, which are agents that promote scar formation and facilitate wound healing. Consequently, enzymes used in wound and ulcer therapy belong to the larger group of cicatricant preparations, highlighting their role within the spectrum of therapeutic agents aimed at promoting tissue repair.

Their combined physiological effects—antioxidant activity, enzyme modulation, cellular metabolism, lipid regulation and bone‑protective actions—underscore their importance for nutritional adequacy, metabolic health and patient‑centered care.

This relationship highlights that enzymatic formulations belong to the broader category of wound‑and‑ulcer treatments, emphasizing their role within the spectrum of medical preparations aimed at promoting healing and tissue repair.

Vitamin D is repeatedly incorporated in multivitamin tablets and liquids and is pharmacologically defined as an inhibitor of bone resorption, underscoring its relevance to osteoporosis management. Collectively, the community’s knowledge depicts an integrated formulation strategy in which fat‑soluble vitamins (A, D, E) provide antioxidant and bone‑protective effects, water‑soluble B‑vitamins (niacin, pantothenic acid) support cellular metabolism and energy production, and calcium pantothenate additionally contributes to wound repair and bone health, reflecting coordinated approaches to metabolic health, skin protection, wound healing and skeletal integrity in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 2 -- Community 925
retrieved_docs.content: Other cicatrizants in ATC is a therapeutic category that includes the agents dexpantenol, becaplermin, and trafermin, each of which is considered a member of this group of wound‑healing and scar‑treatment medications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 332
retrieved_docs.content: Becaplermin and trafermin are both classified as members of the Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) category “Other cicatrizants in ATC,” indicating that they belong to the same therapeutic class of agents used to promote wound healing and tissue repair. This shared classification highlights their similar pharmacologic role as cicatrizant drugs within the ATC system, aiding clinicians in recognizing their comparable indications and therapeutic applications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2079
retrieved_docs.content: Proteolytic enzymes for treatment of wounds and ulcers are classified as members of the kolagenasy group, indicating that they function as collagenases that degrade collagen in wound tissue and thereby facilitate debridement and healing.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2651
retrieved_docs.content: Superficial injury refers to a minor form of trauma that affects the outer layers of tissue, and one specific manifestation of this category is the corneal abrasion known in Arabic as خدش القرنية. This condition represents a superficial injury to the cornea, characterized by a shallow loss of epithelial cells that can cause pain, tearing, and visual disturbance. Recognizing that خدش القرنية is a child concept of superficial injury helps clinicians classify it appropriately within the broader spectrum of minor ocular traumas and guides prompt, targeted management to promote rapid healing and prevent complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 1875
retrieved_docs.content: Other cicatrizants in ATC are classified as members of the trafermin group, indicating that these wound‑healing agents fall within the trafermin category of the Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical classification system.

A functional link between niacin and vitamin E indicates synergistic interactions among fat‑soluble vitamins. Calcium pantothenate (the calcium salt of pantothenic acid, vitamin B5) is an active ingredient in the same multivitamin capsules and tablets that contain niacin, as well as in bulk oral powders. In the Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) system it is classified among “other plain vitamin preparations” and is linked to broader vitamin groups such as vitamin E, benzopyrans and inositol; the ATC subgroup “other cicatrizants” places it within the trafermin class of wound‑healing agents. Its primary mechanism of action is phosphate‑chelating activity, which supports cellular metabolism, enzyme interactions and bone health. Clinically, calcium pantothenate may aid in the prevention of osteoporosis,

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 4 -- Community 2677
retrieved_docs.content: Diabetes (مرض السكري) is clinically associated with several serious complications and related conditions. Toe amputation (بتر أصبع القدم) frequently occurs in patients with diabetes, reflecting the disease’s impact on peripheral vascular health and wound healing. Retinal disorder (اضطراب في الشبكية) is another common clinical manifestation, indicating the risk of diabetic retinopathy and vision loss. In addition, pancreatic disorders that are not themselves diabetes (pancreatic disorders (not diabetes)) are linked to diabetes, underscoring the broader involvement of pancreatic pathology in the disease’s metabolic dysregulation. Moreover, malfunction of the human insulin receptor protein (INSR protein, human) is associated with diabetes, highlighting a genetic and molecular contributor to the condition. Together, these relationships illustrate how diabetes encompasses vascular, ocular, pancreatic, and genetic dimensions that are critical for comprehensive patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 2 -- Community 926
retrieved_docs.content: contains vitamin A as an ingredient, and a topical gel formulation pairs vitamin A with the inactive ingredient salicylic acid 0.02 mg/mg. Collectively, these diverse preparations illustrate the extensive therapeutic relevance of vitamin A across multiple delivery routes. Its capacity to modify cellular activity, engage enzymatic pathways, and act as an antioxidant supports essential functions in vision, immune competence, epithelial integrity, and overall nutritional health, emphasizing the importance of appropriate vitamin A inclusion in patient‑centered supplementation strategies.
